verdict_reasoning: SRC (c-Src) is a non-receptor protein tyrosine kinase that lacks a transmembrane domain and any extracellular domain. It is anchored exclusively to the cytoplasmic (inner) leaflet of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation, with all functional domains (SH4, SH3, SH2, kinase) facing the cytoplasm. No extracellular face is present at any stage of its lifecycle. Pre-no checklist: (1) cell_state_induced — no documented cancer/stress/apoptosis outer-leaflet ecto-pool for SRC protein body; (2) tissue_restricted_surface — no germline/developmental outer-leaflet display documented; (3) lysosomal_exocytosis — SRC is not a lysosomal TM protein; (4) dual_localization — SRC does cycle between PM, endosomes, and cytosol, but always on the inner leaflet; no PM pool is extracellularly accessible; (5) stable_surface_attachment — SRC is not secreted and has no wash-resistant tethering to an extracellular or outer-leaflet scaffold. No cell-surface-directed therapeutic (antibody/ADC/CAR-T) programs target SRC directly on the cell surface; all clinical programs (dasatinib, bosutinib, etc.) are intracellular small-molecule inhibitors. Verdict: inner_leaflet_anchored / no.
